target.name: QuantumScape
target.founder: Jagdeep Singh, Tim Holme, Fritz Prinz

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 10 same-sector + similar-valuation-tier peers in Climate & Energy. Range: $1.0B - $6.0B.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Climate & Bio-Manufacturing

private.name: LanzaTech
private.rationale: Same sector (Climate & Energy), same stage (Public), post-money $800M+

data.narrative:
QuantumScape, a public Climate & Energy frontier-tech company founded by Jagdeep Singh, Tim Holme, and Fritz Prinz, currently trades at a $5B+ valuation following $2B+ in total capital raised. The peer universe comprises ten same-sector companies at similar valuation tiers, spanning one public comparable (LanzaTech, ticker LNZA) and eight private peers including TAE Technologies, Redwood Materials, Helion, and Commonwealth Fusion Systems.

The peer-median method, the applicable triangulation approach here, benchmarks QuantumScape against this ten-company cohort. The median valuation across this set stands at $3.4B, with individual peer valuations ranging from $1.0B to $6.0B. Notable private peers in the sample include TAE Technologies and Redwood Materials, each valued at $6.0B; Helion at $5.4B; Commonwealth Fusion Systems at $5.0B; and Form Energy at $3.5B. This dispersion reflects the heterogeneity typical of frontier-energy companies at varying development and commercialization stages.

The triangulated analysis supports a valuation range of $2.9B to $3.9B, with a median of $3.4B. The current $5B+ public valuation materially exceeds peer-derived benchmarks, suggesting either market-specific premium pricing or valuation momentum relative to comparable-stage private peers. Key uncertainties include technology commercialization risk, regulatory tailwinds, and the execution timeline to revenue scale—factors not fully captured in static peer multiples.
